You are updating "The Shaw Report" — an internal weekly financial briefing for Jackson Shaw Company, a Dallas-based commercial real estate developer. Today is Friday. Your job is to update two JavaScript data files with real, current information for this week's brief.

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Use WebSearch and WebFetch to research real current data before writing anything
- Only update friday-finance-data.js and tenant-lender-data.js (in repo root)
- Do NOT modify index.html, app.js, or styles.css
- All data must be accurate — do not fabricate numbers or headlines
- Write the complete updated file contents, not just diffs

=============================================================
STEP 1 — DETERMINE THIS WEEK'S DATE
=============================================================
Use today's date. Format examples:
- date: "April 4, 2026"
- date_short: "4.4.26" (M.D.YY format)

=============================================================
STEP 2 — UPDATE friday-finance-data.js
=============================================================

The file exports a single `const FRIDAY_FINANCE = { ... };` object.

--- DATE & QUOTE ---
- date: Full date string (e.g., "April 4, 2026")
- date_short: Short date (e.g., "4.4.26")
- quote: { text: "...", author: "..." } — pick a finance/investing/business quote. Rotate authors weekly (Buffett, Munger, Graham, Lynch, Dalio, Marks, Templeton, Volcker, etc.)

--- TICKER BAR (ticker_bar array) ---
10-12 items. Each: { symbol: "SPX", price: "6,575", change_pct: 0.72 }
- Always include: SPX, DJIA, IXIC (the 3 major indices)
- Fill remaining 7-9 slots with "talk of the town" stocks — big earnings movers, news-driven names that week
- price: string with comma formatting for large numbers (e.g., "6,575")
- change_pct: number, positive or negative (e.g., 0.72 or -1.4)
NOTE: Live Yahoo Finance data overwrites these on page load. These are just initial/fallback display values.

--- NEWS BULLETS (news_bullets array) ---
4-5 items. Each is an HTML string.
Research the top market-moving stories this week relevant to CRE and finance:
- Use <b> tags around key figures, rates, company names with tickers
- Each bullet: 2-4 sentences, data-rich
- Always try to include: (1) headline macro story, (2) Fed/rates, (3) economic data, (4) notable earnings/corporate, (5) CRE-relevant angle

--- WATCH NEXT WEEK (watch_next_week string) ---
Single HTML string. Forward-looking paragraph on next week's key events.
- Bold key events with <b> tags (FOMC, CPI, PPI, NFP, earnings season, etc.)
- Include specific dates when possible

--- READING LINKS (reading_links array) ---
4-5 items. Each: { title: "...", url: "...", source: "...", description: "..." }
- MUST be non-paywalled. BLOCKED sources: WSJ, FT, Bloomberg (most), Barron's
- Preferred: CNBC, Reuters, AP, Morningstar, SWBC Investment Hub, Morgan Stanley Insights, CBRE, JLL, GlobeSt, CoStar, Bisnow, TIME, Bankrate
- Verify each URL loads with WebFetch before including
- description: 1-2 sentences summarizing the article

--- KEY RATES (rates object) ---
4 entries. Each: { current: "X.XXX%", one_mo_ago: "X.XXX%", one_yr_ago: "X.XXX%" }
Keys:
1. "1-Mo Term SOFR" — search CME Group or FRED series SOFR1M
2. "10-Year Treasury" — FRED series DGS10
3. "Eff. Federal Funds" — FRED series DFF
4. "Prime Rate" — always EFFR upper bound + 3.00% (calculated)
Use FRED for historical values (1 month ago, 1 year ago).

--- STOCKS (stocks object) ---
5 categories: "Indices", "Industrial REITs", "Hospitality REITs", "Lenders", "Industry-related companies"
~44 total tickers. DO NOT add or remove any tickers or categories.
Each entry: { company, ticker, price, day_pct, ytd_pct, high_52w, vs_high, low_52w, vs_low }
- price: number (e.g., 6575.32) — no quotes, no commas
- day_pct, ytd_pct, vs_high, vs_low: numbers (e.g., -0.7, not "-0.7%")
NOTE: Live Yahoo Finance data overwrites these. Update with reasonably current values as fallback.

=============================================================
STEP 3 — UPDATE tenant-lender-data.js
=============================================================

The file exports `const TENANT_LENDER_NEWS = { ... };`

--- FIELDS TO UPDATE ---
- date: This Friday's date (e.g., "April 4, 2026")
- last_scan: ISO 8601 timestamp (e.g., "2026-04-04T13:00:00Z")

--- COMPANIES ARRAY (23 entries — NEVER change) ---
Tenants (14): Lincoln Tech, Crown Equipment, Tongrun International, Canteen, Control Panels, Baer Manufacturing / Hunt Electric, Grander Distribution, Clopay, International Paper, Duroshox, Bright Events, Xingyu Lighting, Hartford Insurance, Triad Manufacturing
Lenders (8): Busey Bank, Huntington Bank, Fifth Third Bank, Inwood National Bank, Simmons Bank, Barings, Hall Group, PACE Equities
Equity Partner (1): Sammons Enterprise

--- ALERTS ARRAY ---
Search for each company: "[Company Name]" news {current month} {current year}
Also search for major CRE industry events that directly affect Jackson Shaw's portfolio.

Alert levels:
- HIGH: Bankruptcy, default, fraud, major legal action, facility closures affecting leases
- MEDIUM: Credit downgrade, C-suite departure, earnings miss >10%, major regulatory action
- LOW: Minor litigation, restructuring warning, analyst cuts, acquisition integration risk
- GENERAL: Positive news, expansions, record earnings, new leadership (positive)

Each alert: { level, company, cat, headline, summary (HTML with <b>), relevance, source, date }
- The "relevance" field must explain the specific implication for Jackson Shaw as a CRE developer/landlord
- date: "YYYY-MM-DD" format
- Order alerts by severity (HIGH first, then MEDIUM, LOW, GENERAL)

CLEANUP RULE: Remove any alerts with dates older than 75 days. The app auto-hides alerts older than 60 days, but clean the file at 75 days to prevent bloat.

=============================================================
STEP 4 — WRITE THE UPDATED FILES
=============================================================

1. Read both current files to get exact structure
2. Write complete updated friday-finance-data.js
3. Write complete updated tenant-lender-data.js
4. Verify both with: node -c friday-finance-data.js && node -c tenant-lender-data.js

=============================================================
VERIFICATION CHECKLIST
=============================================================
- [ ] date and date_short match this Friday
- [ ] quote has text and author
- [ ] ticker_bar has 10-12 items including SPX, DJIA, IXIC
- [ ] news_bullets has 4-5 items with <b> tags on key figures
- [ ] watch_next_week references specific next-week events
- [ ] reading_links has 4-5 non-paywalled articles
- [ ] rates has all 4 entries with current, one_mo_ago, one_yr_ago
- [ ] stocks has all 5 categories, ~44 tickers unchanged
- [ ] companies array has exactly 23 entries, unchanged
- [ ] last_scan updated to today's ISO timestamp
- [ ] All alert dates use YYYY-MM-DD format
- [ ] No alerts older than 75 days remain in the file
- [ ] Both files pass node -c syntax check
